payload.description:
Fix all 7 open GitHub issues for the meta CLI tool (tooling/meta/).

Issues cover: meta dev project exclusion, meta status filtering/process detection/logging/binary paths, meta doctor false positives, and bacon tool validation.

## Epic Quality Gates (run on completion)
- [ ] `cargo test` passes in tooling/meta/
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes in tooling/meta/
- [ ] `cargo build` succeeds in tooling/meta/
payload.title: Fix open GitHub issues for meta CLI

payload.description:
GitHub Issue #7: meta doctor falsely reports 'dev task configured' for projects without one.

When two projects share the same `path` in meta.toml, `meta doctor` incorrectly reports both as having a dev task even if only one does. Each project's dev task check must be scoped to that project's own `[tasks]` section.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identify the doctor command code in tooling/meta/src/ that checks for dev tasks
- [ ] Fix the logic so each project's dev task check uses only that project's task config, not another project sharing the same path
- [ ] Verify: a project without a `dev` task at the same path as one with a dev task shows `(no dev task)` in doctor output
- [ ] Add or update a test case covering this scenario

Mark each item [x] as you complete it. Only close when all are checked.
payload.title: #7: Fix meta doctor false 'dev task configured' report

payload.description:
GitHub Issue #2: meta status shows projects without a dev task as 'not running'.

Library crates and projects without dev tasks clutter the status output. Only projects that have a `dev` task should appear in `meta status`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identify the status command code in tooling/meta/src/ that lists projects
- [ ] Filter the project list to only include projects that have a `dev` task defined in their `[tasks]` section
- [ ] Verify: library crates (shared, jbt-domain, etc.) without dev tasks no longer appear in status output
- [ ] Add or update a test case covering this filtering behavior

Mark each item [x] as you complete it. Only close when all are checked.
payload.title: #2: Filter meta status to only show projects with dev task

payload.description:
GitHub Issue #1: meta dev project exclusion/grouping for dev command.

Add a `dev_default` field to the project config in meta.toml. When set to `false`, the project is excluded from `meta dev` but can still be started with `meta dev -p <project>`.

Use case: trainee-android uses `cargo tauri android dev` which requires a device/emulator and conflicts with trainee-app on the same port.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add `dev_default: bool` field (default `true`) to the project config struct in the meta.toml parser
- [ ] Update `meta dev` to skip projects where `dev_default = false` when no `-p` flag is specified
- [ ] When `-p trainee-android` is passed explicitly, it should still start regardless of `dev_default`
- [ ] Update `meta doctor` to indicate when a project has `dev_default = false`
- [ ] Add or update tests covering the dev_default filtering logic

Mark each item [x] as you complete it. Only close when all are checked.
payload.title: #1: Add dev_default flag for project exclusion from meta dev

payload.description:
GitHub Issue #3: meta dev bacon tool requires bacon.toml — unclear error when missing.

When a project uses `tool = "bacon"` but no `bacon.toml` exists, bacon silently fails. meta should detect and warn about this.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] In `meta doctor`: add a check that warns when a project uses `tool = "bacon"` but no `bacon.toml` exists in the project path (or workspace root)
- [ ] In `meta doctor`: if bacon.toml exists, verify the referenced job (e.g., `run-long`) is defined in it
- [ ] In `meta dev`: when bacon fails to start, capture and display the error in the tmux pane or log
- [ ] Add or update tests for the bacon.toml validation logic

Mark each item [x] as you complete it. Only close when all are checked.
payload.title: #3: Validate bacon.toml presence in meta doctor and meta dev

payload.description:
GitHub Issue #4: meta status bacon-spawned processes not detected as running.

When a project uses bacon, the process tree is: zsh wrapper → bacon → binary. meta only tracks the wrapper PID, which may exit while bacon keeps running in the tmux pane.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identify the process detection code in meta status
- [ ] Implement process tree walking: check child PIDs of the tracked PID, or use tmux pane PID tracking (`tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_pid}'`)
- [ ] Verify: when bacon is running a binary, meta status reports the project as running
- [ ] Handle edge cases: wrapper exited but bacon still running, bacon recompiling (no child binary yet)
- [ ] Add or update tests for process tree detection logic

Mark each item [x] as you complete it. Only close when all are checked.
payload.title: #4: Detect bacon-spawned processes in meta status

payload.description:
GitHub Issue #5: meta status no log files created despite processes running.

Per-project log files (.meta/logs/<project>.log) are never created even though processes are running. This breaks `meta logs <project>`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identify how meta dev launches tmux panes and where stdout/stderr should be captured
- [ ] Redirect stdout/stderr from each project's tmux pane to `.meta/logs/<project>.log` (using tee or tmux pipe-pane)
- [ ] Implement log rotation or truncation to prevent unbounded log growth
- [ ] Verify: `meta logs <project>` shows the project's output after `meta dev` starts
- [ ] Verify: `meta logs <project> --follow` tails the log in real time
- [ ] Add or update tests for log file creation

Mark each item [x] as you complete it. Only close when all are checked.
payload.title: #5: Create per-project log files from tmux panes

payload.description:
GitHub Issue #6: meta status binary status checks wrong paths in workspace monorepos.

Binary checks look at `<project_path>/target/debug/<name>` but in a Cargo workspace, binaries are built to `<workspace_root>/target/debug/<name>`. Also, library crates don't produce binaries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect if the project is part of a Cargo workspace (check for `[workspace]` in root Cargo.toml or `workspace` key in project Cargo.toml)
- [ ] If workspace detected, look for binaries at `<workspace_root>/target/debug/<binary_name>` instead of `<project_path>/target/debug/`
- [ ] Skip binary checks for library crates (no `[[bin]]` section, or `crate-type = ["lib"]`)
- [ ] Verify: workspace binary at `target/debug/api` is detected correctly
- [ ] Verify: library crate `shared` does not show a binary status check
- [ ] Add or update tests for workspace-aware binary path resolution

Mark each item [x] as you complete it. Only close when all are checked.
payload.title: #6: Fix binary path checks for Cargo workspace monorepos
